title: Switching identity warning contradicts live missing-table state
description: docs/project_control_board.md says `tables/switching_canonical_identity.csv` is present, while the Switching workstream row explicitly downgrades that anchor because the file is missing. This creates contradictory operational signaling inside the canonical Switching control surface.

title: Relaxation ver3 lacks exact-token coverage in canonicalization controls
description: docs/system_registry.json lists `Relaxation ver3` as the active module token, but tables/module_canonical_status.csv and tables/project_workstream_status.csv only track `Relaxation`. That leaves the authoritative registry token without exact coverage in the canonicalization tables and creates alias ambiguity for future progress tracking.

title: MT coverage workstream still describes the resolved missing-row gap
description: The `mt_canonicalization_coverage` workstream row still says coverage rows were missing, but the row itself exists and `tables/mt_canonicalization_blockers.csv` provides the live blocker inventory. The blocker and next-action narrative is stale and can misdirect maintenance follow-up away from the real MT contract gaps.

title: Unified crossover synthesis elevates non-canonical Aging and Relaxation outputs into paper-ready evidence
description: analysis/unified_dynamical_crossover_synthesis.m describes Relaxation `A(T)` as coming from a canonical audit, cites Aging structural crossover from a canonical collapse sweep, and then promotes the combined story as the strongest paper-ready message. The control board still classifies Aging and Relaxation cross-module outputs as WIP/ADVISORY/NOT_CANONICAL_SOURCE, so this synthesis is using non-closed module outputs as canonical-style evidence.
